content:
Trailer # Available for FREE on Steam!
Maid Mayhem is a 2-4 player party brawler where big buff bald maids race to clean a house. They beat the gunk off the furniture to score points and beat the buff out of eachother to sabotage and steal points. Whoever has the most points at the end of the match wins, and is dubbed THE BEST MAID EVER!
🏆 Award Winning | Level Up Student Showcase
1st place in Player’s Choice, voted on by the public and presented by Ubisoft Toronto.
2nd place in Achievement in Audio, judged by industry professionals and presented by Behaviour Interactive
Patchd Up Games People’s Choice Achievement in Audio   Previous Next My Role # I was the Technical Lead on Maid Mayhem, leading a team of 5 programmers across an 11 person team working with art and design departments.
Wrote and iterated on Technical Design Document, including APIs and UML diagrams. Architected all core systems. Built and iterated on all core gameplay systems: cleaning/combat, player controllers, match lifecycle. Scheduled and tracked weekly tasks using P4 Plan (Hansoft) Maintained the Git Repository I also assisted in animation! I did all the cleaning transitional animations for the house furniture.
title: Maid Mayhem

content: Highlight Reel A quick look at what I’ve been working on.
title: My Work!

content:
Trailer # Available for FREE on Steam!
Hardly Workin’ is a goofy office simulator game where you play as Buddy Inepta, a supreme slacker. You balance your work and fun in a goofy and chaotic office and have to avoid falling asleep on the job by slacking off. Just don’t get caught by the BIG BOSS Karen…
My Role # I was the Producer on Hardly Workin’, as well as a Generalist Programmer, AI Programmer, and Animation Programmer. My tasks involved:
Leading a team of 7 through agile-style production, scheduling tasks using P4 Plan (Hansoft) and holding team meetings/check ins. Architect and implement the Boss and NPC AI behaviour Contributed directly to UI/UX, implementing menus and HUD Implemented all animation systems, VFX, and SFX
title: Hardly Workin'

content:
Teaser Video # This is a character prototype of a 2D Puzzle-Platformer game I work on in my free time. You control a being that can change states of matter between Solid, Liquid, and Gas. Each state has different capabilities and drawbacks that will help the player solve puzzles throughout the level-based gameplay.
As it stands this is just a character prototype showcasing primarily the custom and configurable 2D Softbody I created, but expect more updates!
title: Mind Over Matter

content:
Gameplay Video # In the ironic game Flicker you play as a legally distinct plumber who had heeded a job call to repair a decrepit house. The player must navigate through all three stories, fending off ghosts, retrieving parts, and repairing that floor to progress through the house.
My Role # I was one of two Generalist Programmers on Flicker. My role included:
Animation Programming SFX and VFX Programming Puzzle Design System implementation: Inventory, Repairable Objects, Physics Pickups, Manager Systems
title: Flicker

content:
Demo Video # This was a rough vertical slice individual project I worked on in my second year at Niagara College. It focused on concepts like:
AI Programming Finite State Machines Scene and Data Persistency Optimization & Object Pooling
title: Tank!

title: Contact

content:
Hi, I'm Ethan! Thanks for visiting my portfolio. I grew up in the Niagara Region and am currently still located there! I'm a graduate of Brock University and Niagara College's dual degree GAME Programming program. I specialize in Gameplay Programming, AI Programming, and Animation Programming, though I’ve had to wear many hats. I’m also well versed in general programming, 3D modelling, texturing, rigging, and animation.
My proudest achievement is taking my fourth-year capstone project Maid Mayhem to Level Up Student Showcase 2026, where it placed 1st in Player’s Choice, an award voted on by the public and presented by Ubisoft Toronto.
title: Hi there, I'm Ethan
